description: Hand-verified call-path ground truth for end-to-end flow/architecture questions. Unlike the recall@5 golden set (which scores file retrieval), these feed the agent A/B + LLM judge scorecard: did an agent, helped by pcg, actually trace the real call path? Each flow lists the verified file:line steps, the symbols a correct answer MUST hit, and explicit dynamic boundaries (callbacks, data-driven dispatch) where naive static tracing breaks. `memory_probe_required: true` means the repo must FAIL scripts/memory_probe.sh (model cannot trace it unaided) before it counts toward accuracy — famous repos like the two below are illustrative of the schema and should be replaced with lesser-known repos that pass the probe gate.

flows.question: Trace how an incoming HTTP request to a FastAPI route reaches the user's endpoint function and is turned into a serialized HTTP response. Name the functions in order and the file:line of each.

call_path.note: Per-route handler factory; delegates to get_request_handler(...).
Builds the inner async app(request) Starlette route callback.
The inner async app(request) is the actual Starlette route callback.
Resolves the Depends() graph for the endpoint (called at routing.py:473).
Invokes the user's endpoint coroutine/function (called at routing.py:690).
Validates and serializes the return value against the response_field (called ~routing.py:712).
Constructs the final Response object from the serialized content.

dynamic_boundaries: The inner app(request) (routing.py:398) is registered as a Starlette route callback and invoked by the ASGI server, not called directly from get_route_handler.
Dependency resolution in solve_dependencies is data-driven from the endpoint signature (the Depends() graph), so concrete call targets vary per route.
response_class may be a DefaultPlaceholder resolved at runtime into actual_response_class (routing.py:389-391).

flows.question: Trace how a top-level httpx.get(url) call reaches the network transport layer that actually performs the request. Name the functions in order and the file:line of each.

call_path.note: Top-level convenience wrapper; delegates to request().
Opens a Client in a context manager and calls client.send(...).
Entry to the sync send pipeline.
Auth flow loop; delegates to redirect handling.
Redirect loop; delegates to the single-request sender.
Selects the transport and calls transport.handle_request (client.py:1014).

dynamic_boundaries: _transport_for_url (client.py:1005) picks the transport at runtime from the mounts map, so transport.handle_request dispatches to whichever transport (default HTTPTransport, or a test/mock transport) is mounted.
The async path (AsyncClient.send at _client.py:1594) mirrors this chain with awaited equivalents; a correct trace should note the sync/async split.
